name: Prof. Dr. Frank Glorius
description: Group leader driving research at the intersection of organic chemistry, data science, and machine learning for chemical discovery.

name: Dr. Ajnabiul Hoque
description: Deep learning and reinforcement learning for enantioselective C–H activation reactions, generative AI for molecular design, and ML-guided reaction optimization with experimental validation.

name: Latimah Bustillo Arrechea
description: Former Visiting PhD Student in the group. Current position: University of Lisboa, Lisboa, Portugal.

name: Florian Boser
description: Laboratory automation, high-throughput experimentation, machine learning for reactivity prediction, negative data augmentation and agentic LLM workflows for autonomous chemical discovery.

name: Niklas Dobberstein
description: Interested in applying machine learning to chemistry with a focus on generative models for de novo molecular design and reaction prediction.

name: Tobias Elsbecker
description: Former data scientist in the ML group. Current position: CTO, Cheminnovation GmbH.

name: Niklas Hölter
description: His research focuses on the intersection of (photo-)catalysis, lab automation, data science and computational chemistry to bring digital chemistry into real laboratories.

name: Dr. Felix Katzenburg
description: Former PhD student in the ML group. Current position: University of Toronto. Worked on pyGecko for calibration-free GC-MS/FID quantification and automated high-throughput reaction screening.

name: Dr. Marius Kühnemund
description: Former data scientist in the ML group. Current position: Senior ML Engineer, syte GmbH.

name: Maik Niedziella
description: Focused on developing machine learning strategies for reaction discovery by applying structure elucidation techniques to GC-MS spectra, enabling automated identification of unknown products from complex analytical data.

name: Ard van Noordenne
description: Interested in improving the reliability and sustainability of chemistry through data-driven experiment design in combination with high-throughput experimentation platforms.

name: Philipp Pflüger
description: Former PhD student in the ML group. Current position: CEO, Cheminnovation GmbH.

name: Debanjan Rana
description: Former PhD Student in the group. Worked on energy transfer catalysis and data-driven screening strategies for dearomative cycloadditions. Co-developed the EnTdecker platform.

name: Dr. Frederik Sandfort
description: Former PhD Student in the group. Current position: BASF SE, Ludwigshafen, Germany.

name: Dr. Felix Schäfer
description: Former PhD Student in the group. Current position: Lonza AG.

name: Leon Schlosser
description: Combining data-driven and experimental screening strategies for accelerated discovery in photocatalysis.

name: Rubal Sharma
description: PhD student in the Glorius group working on machine learning for organic chemistry.

name: Jan Spies
description: I am working to make organic chemistry literature data more usable for predictive modeling. To do this, I apply Positive-Unlabeled learning to train robust models from biased and imbalanced chemical data.

name: Dr. Felix Strieth-Kalthoff
description: Former PhD Student in the group. Current position: Assistant Professor, University of Wuppertal, Faculty of Mathematics and Natural Sciences, Assistant Professorship for Digital Chemistry.

name: Manh Hung Tran
description: Quantum chemical calculations for mechanistic studies and descriptor generation, combined with chemical space analysis and high-throughput platforms for robustness screening.

name: Nick Wierich
description: Committed to making synthetic chemistry smarter and faster using machine learning.
